channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Practical Guide to Managing College Expenses
item.description: Living independently during college is an important step toward financial responsibility. This article offers a detailed guide to managing essential expenses, including rent, utilities, groceries, tra...

item.title: Palestinian Runner Overcomes Prison Detention to Complete West Bank Marathon
item.description: Mohamad Al-Assi, a 27-year-old Palestinian runner recently released from Israeli administrative detention, marked a significant personal milestone by completing the Palestine Marathon. Held for the fi...

item.title: Fifteen Feared Dead as Gold Mine Collapses in Ramos, West Pokot; Rescue Ongoing
item.description: A tragic incident has hit Ramos area in Kacheliba Constituency, West Pokot County, after a gold mining shaft collapsed on the evening of Thursday, May 7, leaving at least 15 people feared dead. Dozens...

item.title: Two Missing Nairobi Children Found Safe, Father Shares Update
item.description: In Nairobi’s Kariobangi South, a worried family was overjoyed after their two young children, Ephraim, aged four, and Shammar, aged two, went missing on Wednesday, May 6, 2026. The kids had been left ...

item.title: Intergenerational economic challenges and the role of public policy in Australia
item.description: Jack Waterford examines the widely held notion that Baby Boomers are solely responsible for the economic hardships faced by younger Australians. While Boomers benefited from post-war prosperity, free ...

item.title: TVK and AMMK Dispute Over MLA Support Letter in Tamil Nadu Assembly Formation
item.description: In Tamil Nadu, political drama intensified after the state assembly elections resulted in a hung assembly. Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK), which is one MLA short of the majority mark of 118 in...

item.title: Rail Revival Key to Easing Cape Town's Road Congestion
item.description: Cape Town has been struggling with heavy road congestion, ranking as the third most congested city in Africa in 2025, slightly better than its position in 2023. Data from the TomTom Traffic Index show...

item.title: Decomposed body of missing Limpopo teen discovered, police continue search for brother
item.description: In Limpopo, the dismembered body of 18-year-old Anna Ramollo, a wheelchair-bound woman from Rakgoatha village, was discovered in a shallow grave near Polokwane. She had been missing since May 1, along...

item.title: Birmingham City Council sees Labour replaced after 14 years amid gains by Reform, Greens and independents
item.description: After 14 years of Labour leadership, Birmingham City Council has shifted towards a more fragmented political landscape. In the recent elections, no single party achieved an overall majority, highlight...

item.title: Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani to take charge as India’s Chief of Defence Staff
item.description: The Union government on Saturday announced the appointment of Lieutenant General NS Raja Subramani as the next Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) of India. Subramani, who retired as Vice Chief of the Indian...

item.title: Victoria police officer charged with sexual assault related to off-duty incident
item.description: A Victoria police officer, Constable Chris Kayiatos, has been charged with sexual assault following an external investigation by the West Shore RCMP. The B.C. Prosecution Service approved the charge a...

item.title: Adamu Atiku Joins Adamawa South Senatorial Race with ADC
item.description: Adamu Atiku, son of former Vice President Atiku Abubakar, don officially enter the race to represent Adamawa South in the Senate. He don buy the Expression of Interest and Nomination Form from the Afr...

item.title: NECO to Begin Computer-Based Exams in 2026 with Hybrid System for Students
item.description: Starting May/June 2026, NECO don announce say dem go begin use Computer-Based Testing (CBT) for Senior Secondary Certificate Examination (SSCE), as dem wan phase out paper exams. For the first stage, ...

item.title: Mobile Skin Cancer Clinic Provides Essential Dermatology Services in Windsor and Chatham
item.description: Windsor and Chatham residents now have improved access to dermatology services thanks to a new mobile skin cancer clinic. The initiative aims to bridge the growing gap in dermatology care in these are...

item.title: Barrie Drewitt-Barlow: Life, Surrogacy, and Recent Legal Charges

item.title: Reform UK makes notable gains in local elections, challenging Labour and Conservative strongholds
item.description: Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, achieved significant advances in the 2026 local elections across England and Wales, marking a noteworthy development in British politics. The party secured control of E...

item.title: ACL Injuries in Australia: Surgery vs Rehabilitation Outcomes
item.description: Anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injuries are common in Australian sports such as Aussie rules football, rugby league and basketball. Thousands of Australians rupture their ACLs each year, often exper...

item.title: Hank Azaria shares insights into his unique personal hobby
item.description: In this feature, the focus is on actor Hank Azaria exploring a hobby that offers a surprising glimpse into his personal life, contrasting with his well-known screen persona. The article highlights how...

item.title: Limpopo Police Denounce False Social Media Claims About Missing Toddler
item.description: Limpopo police have strongly condemned the spread of false information regarding the disappearance of two-year-old Omphile Sethole. The toddler went missing from her home in Ga-Mabuela village during ...

item.title: BJP Legislature Party to Choose Assam Chief Minister on May 10
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) in Assam is preparing to appoint a new Chief Minister following recent political developments in the state. The BJP Legislature Party has scheduled a meeting on May 10...
